raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"I tried to break it up into little chunks, but it just felt like I was constantly falling short; I ended up staying up until midnight and still didn’t feel anything was good enough. I didn’t do anything else to cope, so the exhaustion just fed into the same self‑criticism loop. I keep wondering if this will ever change my chances for a postdoc or even finish the PhD.","problems_referred":["perfectionism","work_stress","career_uncertainty"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: I tried to break it up into little chunks, but it just felt like I was constantly falling short; I ended up staying up until midnight and still didn’t feel anything was good enough. I didn’t do anything else to cope, so the exhaustion just fed into the same self‑criticism loop. I keep wondering if this will ever change my chances for a postdoc or even finish the PhD.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here as a supportive friend, and you need acknowledgment of your efforts and feelings right now. Given your report of trying to break tasks into smaller chunks but still feeling like you're falling short, I think a strategy of support and complex reflection could help. You've mentioned that you ended up staying up until midnight and still didn't feel anything was good enough, which suggests that you're putting a lot of pressure on yourself. I recall from our previous conversations that you've been struggling with self-criticism and the fear of not being good enough, and it seems like this is still a major concern for you. My entry point will be to acknowledge the specific effort you made to break tasks into smaller chunks and how that didn't seem to alleviate your feelings of inadequacy.

evidence_used.content: trying to break tasks into smaller chunks
fear of not being good enough
staying up until midnight and still didn’t feel anything was good enough

parsed.final_response: You tried to break it down into smaller chunks, but it still felt like you were constantly falling short, and that's a really tough spot to be in. It takes a lot of courage to keep going even when it feels like nothing is good enough. I can see how the pressure to perform well for your postdoc chances and to finish your PhD is weighing heavily on you. It might be helpful to explore what you think would need to happen for you to feel like you're good enough, and if there are any smaller, more manageable steps you could take towards that goal.
